# ADR-260004: Implementation boundaries for visual reuse, copy policy, and external integrations

Status: Accepted
Date: 2026-05-21
Owners: Repository owner

## Context

The project has locked site shape and content structure. Implementation now needs explicit boundaries
for what can be reused from the archive, what must be rewritten, and which optional integrations
are allowed at launch.

## Decision

- Archive visuals that are candidates for direct copy (if accurate):
  - brand assets (logo, favicon),
  - product/add-on screenshots or GIFs,
  - Google trademark notice text where relevant.
- Archive visuals that require new implementation:
  - full page layouts,
  - card/grid compositions,
  - typography/button/spacing system,
  - legacy visual patterns from the previous technology stack.
- Copy policy:
  - marketing copy is rewritten,
  - legal/support copy is reviewed and adapted before publication,
  - technical add-on instructions are kept only if still accurate.
- Brand voice direction: modernized, precise, non-hype tone.
- Launch integration policy:
  - The initial launch will focus on core content and functionality, with external integrations like analytics planned for a later phase.
    (Superseded by ADR-260012: GA4 analytics was approved post-launch. The rest of this policy stands.)
  - The contact path will use the dedicated contact page with a `mailto:` fallback.
- Redirect policy:
  - provide compatibility routes/pages for high-value legacy public URLs,
  - prioritize add-on/legal/support route continuity where practical.

## Alternatives Considered

- Port archive visuals and copy mostly as-is: Rejected due to outdated layout system and potential
  content drift.
- Full redesign with no archive asset reuse: Rejected because valid brand assets and media should be
  preserved when accurate.
- Launch with analytics/forms by default: Rejected to keep privacy/compliance and delivery scope
  simple during initial public release.

## Consequences

- Positive: Implementation remains bounded and consistent with static-site objectives.
- Positive: Lower risk of stale UI patterns or inaccurate marketing claims.
- Negative: More manual copy editing and visual QA effort.
- Risk: Legacy links may break if compatibility routes are missed.

## Verification

- No Angular/CoreUI-driven layout patterns are present in the new site.
- The new site will be implemented with a modern layout system, avoiding legacy patterns
- Legal/support pages show reviewed content before release.
- Launch build excludes analytics scripts and form-backend dependencies unless approved.
- The launch build will include only dependencies required for core static-site functionality

## Rollback

If these boundaries prove too strict, adjust with a follow-up ADR and add a scoped backlog item for
the exception (e.g., approved analytics integration).

## Links

- Related requirement(s): REQ-FR-260004.md
- Related backlog item(s): BI-260004.md
- Superseded in part by: ADR-260012.md (analytics-launch clause only; all other boundaries remain Accepted)
